data.description: Core data layer — models, lookup tables, helpers

calc.description: Calculation engine — chart computation, patterns, profiles

ui.description: UI components — Streamlit sidebar widgets, forms

mcp.description: MCP pipeline — topic maps, reading engine, LLM synthesis

data.label: _sign_index

data.docstring: Return sign name from absolute degree.

data.label: calculate_house_cusps
data.docstring:
Return house cusp rows.

- If house_system in {"placidus","equal","whole"}: returns 12 rows for that system.
- If house_system is None or "all": returns 36 rows (Placidus, Equal, Whole), each labeled.

data.docstring:

data.label: circuit_sim
data.docstring:
circuit_sim.py — Circuit Power Simulation Engine
=================================================
Models an astrological chart as an electrical circuit:
  • Planets/points  →  power sources (voltage = power_index from dignity_calc)
  • Aspect edges    →  conductive wires (ASPECT_CONDUCTANCE values)…

data.label: _conductance_for
data.docstring: Return the conductance value for an aspect type, defaulting to 0.5.

data.label: _build_node
data.docstring: Construct a CircuitNode, seeding power values from PlanetaryState.

data.label: _mutual_reception_names
data.docstring: Flatten mutual reception pairs into a set of planet names.

data.label: _bfs_reroute
data.docstring:
BFS through conductive_edges (excluding the source-target quincunx itself)
to find the shortest alternative path from source to target.

Returns a list of node names forming the path (inclusive of end…

data.label: _apply_mutual_reception_boost
data.docstring:
Boost effective_power on mutual reception nodes and clamp edge conductance
upward for edges connecting them.  Mutates in place.

data.label: _apply_sn_nn_boost
data.docstring:
Boost conductance on edges that lie along the SN→NN path.
Mutates edges in place.

data.label: _propagate_power
data.docstring:
Run one pass of power propagation across all edges.
Mutates CircuitNode and CircuitEdge objects in place.

data.label: _find_sn_nn_path_in_simulation
data.docstring:
BFS on all nodes (across all shapes) to find the shortest conductive path
from any South Node variant to any North Node variant.

Returns the path as a list of planet names, or [] if no path exists.

data.label: _simulate_shape
data.docstring:

shape dict keys used:

    type     (str)
    members  (List[str])
    edges…

The purpose of this module is to demonstrate how the application can be changed

information from the database.  You can call ``load_static_from_db()`…

data.label: calculate_quality_index

Sigmoid normalization of raw authority.

QI = tanh(raw_authority / 7)

  - Strong debil…

data.label: calculate_power_index

Combined planetary strength magnitude.

PI = sqrt((|QI| * 0.4)^2 + (P * 0.6)^2) * mu

Each row encodes one canonical concept with its alias patterns,
optional astrological factors, routing intent, domain grouping, and
a short descript…

data.label: test_circuit_sim
data.docstring:
test_circuit_sim.py — Integration tests for the circuit power simulation engine.

Runs without a live ephemeris or database connection.
Constructs minimal mock chart objects and shape dicts to verify:
  1. CircuitNode/Edge building from shape dicts
  2. Power propagation (conductance multiplication)…

data.label: _make_chart_minimal
data.docstring: Return a minimal AstrologicalChart with no real ephemeris data.

data.docstring: Quick integration test for the planetary strength engine.

data.docstring: Quick integration smoke test for the circuit-driven MCP chatbot pipeline.

data.docstring: Voice toggle produces different system prompts.

tests/test_chart_serializer.py
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
Verify that chart_serializer produces valid, JSON-safe output with all
expected fields present.

data.label: _make_minimal_chart
data.docstring: Build a minimal AstrologicalChart with Sun, Moon, Saturn for testing.

data.label: make_chart
data.docstring: Helper: create an AstrologicalChart from list of dict rows.

data.label: test_no_divider_for_ac_dc
data.docstring: AC and DC should not produce an empty house section divider in default mode.

data.label: test_focus_mode_skips_house_for_ac_dc
data.docstring: When interpreting a single AC or DC in focus mode there should be no house block.

data.label: test_rules_line_appears_without_chart_objects
data.docstring:
Even if there are no other chart objects in the ruled signs/houses, the
'Rules:' line should still appear listing the sign/house.

data.label: test_rules_line_includes_objects_when_present
data.docstring:
When other objects occupy a ruled sign or house, they should be listed
in parentheses next to the sign/house name.

data.label: test_static_db_exposes_legacy_constants
data.docstring:
Several lists and maps that used to live in lookup_v2 should now be
accessible directly on the ``static_db`` object and reflect the same
values as the old module.  This verifies the compatibility shim…

data.label: test_optional_db_migration
data.docstring:
If a PostgreSQL database is configured, run the migration script and do a simple sanity check.

This test is skipped silently when the necessary environment variables are not defined.

data.label: test_db_completeness
data.docstring:
Verify the database rows mirror the in-memory lookup data.

Runs only when PGDATABASE is set.

data.label: _apply_transit_offset
data.docstring: Shift transit_dt_utc forward (+1) or backward (-1) by the selected interval.

data.label: _set_transit_now
data.docstring: Reset transit time to the current moment and recalculate.

data.label: _apply_direct_transit_date
data.docstring:
Apply the direct date/time inputs from the expander to transit_dt_utc.

The user enters date/time in the location's local timezone; convert to UTC
before storing.

data.label: _render_transit_date_nav
data.docstring: Render the transit date navigator: fwd/back buttons, interval dropdown, date display, and direct input expander.

data.label: _canon_label
data.docstring: Prefer the display name your app uses; fall back to alias if needed.

data.label: _resolve_present_targets
data.docstring:
Return (present, missing) after normalizing aliases (e.g., 'MC' -> 'Midheaven').
We keep a max of 6 already by curation, but also dedupe.

data.label: apply_wizard_targets
data.docstring:
Flip the per-object 'singleton_' checkboxes and set focus to the first present target.
Also ensures the Compass Rose overlay is on for orientation.
